_doc: Reviewed-honest string literals that contain an over-claim term but are NOT viewer-facing provenance claims. The over-claim gate (scripts/helpers/over-claim-scan.mjs, called by verify-phase0.mjs) fails on any asserting over-claim literal NOT listed here, forcing human review of additions. Each entry needs a reason. Match is exact string equality against the scanned literal's inner content.

allow.reason: sourceTier enum comparison value in code (station.sourceTier === 'operator-validated'); gates the Tier-1 badge, not rendered prose.
Tier-1 authority-class badge label; renders ONLY when a pair genuinely is operator-validated (legacy CHT x Speedcast fixture). Correct authority naming, not a claim about a modeled value.

allow.match: Measured operation, service-level guarantee, radio-layer switch trace, or real route.
allow.reason: evidenceBoundary column of a truthBoundary non-claim row = the upper bound of what the report explicitly CANNOT prove.

allow.match: Measured service KPI, packet trace, or customer acceptance evidence.
allow.reason: evidenceBoundary of the metric-anchor row = explicitly NOT proven by the report.
TypeScript union-type member / mustNotImply category key (names a thing the demo must not imply).
TypeScript union-type member / mustNotImply category key (names a thing the demo must not imply).
Item inside a mustNotImply array (tier-1 fixture projection): the thing the demo must NOT imply.
Item inside a mustNotImply array (tier-1 fixture projection): the thing the demo must NOT imply.

allow.match: Measured communication-rate visualization beyond route F-09 class
allow.reason: Value of a gap: field declaring an external (not-yet-available) capability; hookpoint text states 'measurement truth remains external'.
